response.justification: Strong senior engineer with 15+ years of experience, solid open-source background, Linux/Python-adjacent expertise, and proven craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, architecture). However, the role demands C1 German and the candidate self-reports only Basic German, which is a hard blocker for a client-facing leadership position in the DACH market. Formal team-leadership/Head-of-Development experience is also thin — mostly senior IC roles with mentoring and workshop facilitation rather than direct managerial oversight.
strengths: 15+ years of polyglot engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and distributed systems with strong open-source and Linux affinity
Proven architecture, TDD, and craftsmanship culture-building (workshops, dojos, courses, founder of GISCollective)
Track record advising and delivering across consulting-style engagements with diverse stacks and clients
concerns: German listed as Basic — falls far short of the required C1 proficiency
No explicit Head-of-Development or formal people-management experience; leadership signal is mentorship/community, not org leadership
Python and UML are not prominently demonstrated; primary stacks are TS/Node and DLang

key_factors.factor: Senior engineering breadth and open-source/Linux fit
